rules.exact_text: Apply generously and evenly on all exposed skin.

rules.exact_text: 50 mg/1 mL
rules.reference: Proposed change 2R.5 (TGO 91 11(2)(f)) - Permitted alternative expression for 1 mL injections (with preference for 'in' wording where space allows)

rules.exact_text: For components of injectable nutritional therapy that are not electrolytes, show the quantity of active ingredients as the stated weight of the active ingredient in the stated volume of fill in the container.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 2R.9

rules.exact_text: Where potassium is the principal active ingredient in an injectable medicine, at least the active ingredient and quantity must be in red font.
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 2R.12 (Section 10)

TGO 91/TGO 92 (active ingredients and quantity of active ingredients appear as cohesive unit on main label)
TGO 91 (prescription medicines must display name of the medicine on 3 non-opposing sides of a carton)

rules.exact_text: Swallow with water (or words to that effect)
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 5.3 Direction to swallow with water proposal

rules.exact_text: If a QR code contains a number or link unique to the unit (serialised), the label must also include a DataMatrix code that contains the serial number (per TGO 106)
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 6.1 QR codes; Therapeutic Goods (Medicines-Standard for Serialisation and Data Matrix Codes) (TGO 106) Order 2021
TGO 91 and TGO 92 section 8 (batch number and expiry date required on labels)
TGO 91 and TGO 92 section 7 (batch number may be embossed/debossed; in that instance no colour contrast required)
Proposed new standard replacing TGO 92 (sunscreens usage instructions)

rules.exact_text: Do not spray/ apply directly to the face. Spray/ apply onto hands and then apply to the face.
rules.reference: Proposed new standard replacing TGO 92 (sunscreens dosage-form specific statements)

rules.exact_text: Apply out of the wind.
rules.reference: Proposed new standard replacing TGO 92 (sunscreens dosage-form specific statements)

rules.exact_text: For medicines quantified in micrograms, display 'microgram' in full unless it does not fit on the label and the label is on a small (TGO 92) or small/very small (TGO 91) container.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 2R.7

rules.exact_text: Medicines subject to TGO 92 with large solid oral dosage forms must include an image of the dosage form if the container is not transparent (when they exceed certain size limits).
rules.reference: TGO 92 proposed change 5R.2

rules.exact_text: Batch number identifier/prefix must accompany (not necessarily precede) the batch number.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 7R.2 (Section 6/8)

rules.exact_text: Include 'distributed by' or similar when the company on the label is the distributor, not the sponsor.
rules.reference: Planned guidance update 7G.3 (Sponsor or distributor name and contact details)

rules.exact_text: contains almond products
rules.reference: Proposed changes - tree nuts (new standards replacing TGO 91/TGO 92)

rules.exact_text: contains gluten
rules.reference: Current requirement described in guidance (gluten declaration at ≥20 ppm)

rules.exact_text: contains hydroxybenzoates
rules.reference: Schedule 1 of TGO 91/TGO 92 (hydroxybenzoic acid esters declaration requirement)
Chunk 6 (Australia) - Vaccines with ≤3 active ingredients: main label must show names and quantities of each active ingredient
Chunk 6 (Australia) - Vaccine descriptor should be included after medicine name and before active ingredients
TGO 91/TGO 92 section 9 and subsection 9(3) (clarification proposed in consultation paper V1.2, 20 Jan 2026, Chunk 7)
TGO 91/TGO 92 (dosage form name must be included on labels)

rules.exact_text: The label must clearly state that the instructions are in the insert.
rules.reference: TGO 91 paragraph 8(1)(l); TGO 92 paragraph 8(1)(m)
Proposed change (consultation text): for vitamin active ingredients on non-prescription labels, require both common name and approved name

rules.exact_text: Apply generously to the skin 20 minutes before skin exposure, then reapply frequently, and after swimming, sweating or towelling.
rules.reference: Proposed new standard replacing TGO 92 (sunscreens usage instructions)

rules.exact_text: Shake well before use.
rules.reference: Proposed new standard replacing TGO 92 (sunscreens dosage-form specific statements)

rules.exact_text: For injectable solutions intended for single use with a total stated volume of 1 mL, express the active ingredient quantity as the stated amount in the total volume (e.g., '50 mg in 1 mL', not '50 mg/mL').
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 2R.5

rules.exact_text: Where potassium is the principal active ingredient in an injectable medicine, at least the name of the medicine must be in red font.
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 2R.12 (Section 10)

rules.exact_text: Listed medicines must prominently display all warning statements.
rules.reference: TGO 92 proposed change 5R.3 (Section 8)

rules.exact_text: The 'name of the medicine' is required on at least 3 non-opposing sides of a carton.
rules.reference: Planned guidance update 3G.3 (Name of the medicine presentation)

rules.reference: Proposed changes - tree nuts (new standards replacing TGO 91/TGO 92)
TGO 91 / TGO 92 (as described in chunk: substance declarations not required on small vial supplied in an outer carton where primary pack includes full declaration)
TGO 91/TGO 92 approach described (prescription medicines: required substance declarations may appear on pack label or in CMI; OTC/listed: on label)

rules.exact_text: If a QR code is displayed on the medicine label, a short statement must be included near the QR code to explain its purpose
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 6.1 Proposed changes - QR codes
TGA guidance (Australia) - machine-readable codes (recommendation, not mandated)

rules.exact_text: For injectable medicines intended for use as a single dose, show the quantity of active ingredients as either (a) the stated weight of the active ingredient in the stated volume of fill in the container, or (b) if the dose is measured in units in clinical practice, as the number of units.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 2R.8

rules.exact_text: Strengths of fixed-dose combination products should be included in the name of the medicine (e.g., 'Name 10/5').
rules.reference: Planned guidance update 3G.1 (Name of the medicine)

rules.exact_text: The identifier should clearly and individually precede the batch number where possible; a combined batch number and expiry date identifier such as 'Lot/Exp' may be acceptable on small and very small containers or where there are other space limitations.
rules.reference: Planned guidance update 7G.1 (Batch number and expiry date identifiers)

rules.exact_text: If the QR code links to a list of excipients, it must be consistent with the ARTG entry and must include all excipients.
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, Version history V1.2 (20 January 2026), section '6.1 QR codes'

rules.exact_text: contains sulfites
rules.reference: Schedule 1 of TGO 91/TGO 92 (sulfites declaration requirement)
Chunk 6 (Australia) - Listed medicines (proposed change): allow active ingredients to appear on side/rear panel when medicine contains at least two active ingredients
Chunk 6 (Australia) - Insulin (planned guidance): display international units per mL prominently near medicine name; encourage largest possible font on individual pens

rules.exact_text: If a QR code is displayed on the medicine label, it must link to a website whose content is controlled by the sponsor and must not link to a website that requires a login, payment, has a geoblock or any other restriction to access the content
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 6.1 Proposed changes - QR codes
TGO 91 paragraph 8(1)(l); TGO 92 paragraph 8(1)(m) (allowed when insufficient label space; label must state instructions are in insert)

rules.exact_text: If a QR code is used and the medicine is a powder for reconstitution, a direction that the medicine must be reconstituted must still be included on the label.
rules.reference: Proposed change (Australia) - instructions for preparation (injectables administered by healthcare professionals)

rules.exact_text: For prescription and related medicines, place the name of the medicine (and cohesive unit) immediately below the signal heading.
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 3R.1

rules.exact_text: Do not use embossed or debossed batch numbers on the primary pack unless they are also printed or clearly contrast with a dark background.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 7R.1 (Section 7)

rules.exact_text: Do not use embossed or debossed expiry dates on the primary pack unless they are also printed or clearly contrast with a dark background.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 7R.1 (Section 7)

rules.exact_text: For injectable vaccines, the name and quantity of each excipient is not required on the primary pack if the full list cannot fit due to the size of the primary pack and the label includes a statement directing the user to the Product Information (PI) for a full list of excipients.
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 7R.4 (10(3))

rules.exact_text: Swallow whole; Do not crush or chew (or equivalent)

rules.exact_text: If a QR code links to a list of excipients, it must be consistent with the ARTG entry and must include all excipients

rules.exact_text: Prescription medicine labels must include a machine-readable code (e.g., linear barcode) that encodes a Global Trade Item Number (GTIN), unless the medicine is a starter pack or meets specific exemptions under TGO 91
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 6.2 Machine-readable code with GTIN

rules.exact_text: Express the quantity of active ingredients that are non-viable biological organisms as the number of non-viable cells present per metric unit for liquids and powders, and as the number of non-viable cells present per dosage unit for other dosage forms.
rules.reference: TGO 92 proposed change 2R.11

rules.exact_text: For prescription and related medicines, the name of the medicine on the label must be unique and, where needed to differentiate (e.g., sponsor/distributor name included in ARTG name), must be included in the label medicine name and presented in a continuous, uninterrupted manner.
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 3R.1

rules.exact_text: The name of the medicine, active ingredients and quantity of active ingredients must appear as a cohesive unit on the main label without interruption of additional information (except in certain circumstances).
rules.reference: TGO 91 subsection 9(3); TGO 92 subsection 9(3)
Chunk 6 (Australia) - Vaccines with ≥4 active ingredients/valences: may omit listing all active ingredient names and quantities if conditions met

rules.exact_text: Warning: large [short name of dosage form] not required for dosage forms not intended to be swallowed whole (e.g., chewable dosage forms and lozenges) if there are clear directions not to swallow them whole
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 5.3 Large dosage form warning statement proposal

rules.exact_text: If a large dosage form warning statement is required and the container is not transparent, include an image of the actual size of the dosage form with the words 'actual size' next to it; position the image next to the warning statement; the image must be on the label and cannot be a sticker on the cap or lid
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 5.3 Image proposal

rules.exact_text: QR codes may appear on medicine labels to give access to more information but do not replace any information legally required to be printed on the label
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 6.1 QR codes

rules.exact_text: Content accessed via a QR code on the label must be consistent with information approved by the TGA for that medicine, must not contradict or misalign with any information on the medicine label, and must not breach the Therapeutic Goods Advertising Code
rules.reference: Medicine labels: Proposed changes to rules for the new standards replacing TGO 91 and TGO 92, V1.2 (20 Jan 2026), Section 6.1 Proposed changes - QR codes

rules.exact_text: If active ingredients and their quantities are included on the side panel, list the name and quantity of each active ingredient on separate lines of text (cohesive unit requirement).
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 2R.13

rules.exact_text: Medicines subject to TGO 92 with large solid oral dosage forms must include a warning statement and a direction to swallow with water if they exceed certain size limits.
rules.reference: TGO 92 proposed change 5R.2

rules.exact_text: Medicines subject to TGO 92 must include on the label either the name of each excipient or a statement explaining how to find excipient information (e.g., via QR code).
rules.reference: TGO 92 proposed change 7R.5

rules.exact_text: Require the purpose of the medicine to be stated on the label for medicines that are 'For Practitioner Dispensing Only'.
rules.reference: TGO 92 s8(1)(n); proposed change 10R.3 (removal of exception in TGO 92 s8(1)(n)(i))

rules.exact_text: See Product Information (PI) for full list of active ingredients (including specific strains/serotypes and quantities).

Chunk 6 (Australia) - If PI not physically included, primary pack label must include QR code or website address linking to current approved PI; link must remain active for shelf life

rules.exact_text: See the Product Information for a full list of excipients
rules.reference: Proposed change (consultation text): injectable vaccines may omit excipient list on primary pack if it cannot fit and label directs user to PI

rules.exact_text: Expiry date identifier/prefix must accompany (not necessarily precede) the expiry date.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 7R.2 (Section 6/8)

rules.exact_text: Include at least one Australian contact detail to facilitate public contact: phone number, email address, or website address (with contact details on the website).
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 7R.3 (Section 6 / 8(1)(i))

rules.exact_text: If a medicine is a concentrated solution for injection in a container over 100 millilitres, include a direction not to administer the solution undiluted.
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 8R.5 (10(2))

rules.exact_text: Listed medicines with a vaginal route of administration must include the statement 'Do not use if pregnant or likely to become pregnant'.
rules.reference: TGO 92 proposed change 8R.6

rules.reference: TGO 91 / TGO 92 Schedule 1 (tree nuts declaration requirement as described)

TGO 91 and TGO 92 section 8 (batch number and expiry date required on labels)
TGO 91 section 8; TGO 92 section 8 (label must show sponsor or distributor name and contact details)

rules.exact_text: Clearly indicate the product is only for dermal application on unbroken skin.
rules.reference: Proposed new standard replacing TGO 92 (sunscreens usage instructions)

rules.exact_text: Hold container 10 to 15 cm away from the body.

rules.exact_text: 50 mg in 1 mL
rules.reference: Proposed change 2R.5 (TGO 91 11(2)(f)) - Example of required expression for 1 mL injections

rules.exact_text: If active ingredients and their quantities are included on the side or rear panel as permitted, the active ingredient name and quantity must not be interrupted with additional information not permitted for the cohesive unit on the main label.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 2R.13 (subsection 9(3))

rules.exact_text: Modified release and enteric coated tablets, capsules and granules for oral administration must include a statement denoting the approved method of administration (e.g., 'Swallow whole') and a warning statement against other methods (e.g., 'Do not crush or chew').
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 5R.1

rules.exact_text: QR codes: include a statement explaining the purpose of the QR code and meet requirements for the linked website and content accessed via the QR code.
rules.reference: TGO 91/TGO 92 proposed change 6R.1

rules.exact_text: Provide space for a dispensing label with a minimum size of 80 x 40 millimetres (exceptions continue where packaging is too small; if full space cannot fit, include a smaller space where possible).
rules.reference: TGO 91 proposed change 7R.6 (8(2))

rules.exact_text: The identifier should clearly and individually precede the expiry date where possible; a combined batch number and expiry date identifier such as 'Lot/Exp' may be acceptable on small and very small containers or where there are other space limitations.
rules.reference: Planned guidance update 7G.1 (Batch number and expiry date identifiers)
